# Issue #8786: fix(autoencoderkl): handle `proj_attn`→`out_proj` key mapping in `load_old_state_dict`
**Repository:** Project-MONAI/MONAI
**Author:** ytl0623
**Status:** closed
**Created At:** 2026-03-19T16:23:10Z

## Description
Fixes #8544 

### Description

Map `proj_attn` → `out_proj` when both exist, initialise `out_proj` to identity/zero when only the new model has it, and silently discard `proj_attn` when only the old checkpoint has it.

### Types of changes
<!--- Put an `x` in all the boxes that apply, and remove the not applicable items -->
- [x] Non-breaking change (fix or new feature that would not break existing functionality).
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or new feature that would cause existing functionality to change).
- [ ] New tests added to cover the changes.
- [ ] Integration tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh -f -u --net --coverage`.
- [ ] Quick tests passed locally by running `./runtests.sh --quick --unittests  --disttests`.
- [ ] In-line docstrings updated.
- [ ] Documentation updated, tested `make html` command in the `docs/` folder.

<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

Updated AutoEncoderKL.load_old_state_dict to handle legacy attention projection keys (`proj_attn.weight`/`proj_attn.bias`) per-block: if the new model has `attn.out_proj.*` and legacy `proj_attn.*` exist, copy them (initialize missing legacy bias to zeros with matching dtype/device); if the new model lacks `attn.out_proj.*` but legacy `proj_attn.*` exist, remove those legacy keys from the old state dict to avoid unexpected-key errors. The method no longer unconditionally sets `out_proj.weight` to identity/ bias to zero. The `verbose` docstring now states it prints diagnostic key-mismatch information. Tests and a helper to synthesize legacy state dicts were added.

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~20 minutes

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

<!-- pre_merge_checks_walkthrough_start -->
